problem_addressed: Existing multi-domain skin lesion segmentation models inadequately utilize prediction uncertainty, leading to blind fusion of artifacts, passive topological reasoning, and overfitting to label noise.
core_claim: UGDD-Net achieves superior segmentation reliability and generalization by actively integrating uncertainty throughout the learning lifecycle via an uncertainty-in-the-loop architecture.
key_contributions: Uncertainty-Guided Bi-directional Feature Fusion (UGBFF) module dynamically modulates spatial-spectral interactions using pixel-level uncertainty
Uncertainty-Guided Graph Refinement (UGGR) module constructs priority-guided topology-aware graphs for active global reasoning
Uncertainty-Guided Margin-Adaptive Loss (UGML) enforces strict constraints on confident pixels while relaxing penalties on uncertain ones
novelty: Transforms uncertainty from a passive post-hoc metric to an active guiding signal through the novel 'Glance-and-Gaze' forward mechanism, enabling uncertainty-permeated learning across all stages.
method_summary: UGDD-Net employs a dual-domain architecture with spatial and frequency branches, enhanced by three uncertainty-guided modules that actively utilize prediction uncertainty for feature fusion, graph-based refinement, and loss adaptation.
key_innovation: The 'Glance-and-Gaze' forward mechanism that actively propagates uncertainty signals throughout the entire learning lifecycle, enabling reliable features to rectify unreliable ones and confident predictions to guide uncertain regions.
assumptions: Pixel-level uncertainty estimates are meaningful and accurately reflect prediction confidence
Dual-domain representations capture complementary spatial and frequency information
Uncertainty can be effectively used to guide both feature fusion and topological reasoning
theoretical_grounding: No formal proofs provided; empirical validation through extensive experiments showing statistically significant improvements (p<0.01) across multiple datasets and metrics, particularly in Expected Calibration Error (ECE) reduction.
headline_result: UGML achieved IoU of 92.61% and Dice of 96.01% on the main dataset, and IoU of 79.92% on Hard Samples
ablations: Comparison of different loss functions: Dice + CE, Dice + CE + Boundary, and UGML
improvement_over_baseline: UGML improved IoU by 0.77%, Dice by 0.49%, HD95 by 0.6, and ECE by 0.23% compared to Loss 3
best_result: UGML achieved IoU of 92.61% and Dice of 96.01% on the main dataset, and IoU of 79.92% on Hard Samples
title: Uncertainty-Guided Dual-Domain Learning for Reliable Skin Lesion Segmentation
